perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins in a crowded Costco warehouse where two women in stylish off-shoulder tops are excitedly holding large, long yellow boxes designed like Harry Potter train tickets. One woman wears a bucket hat. They gesture towards the product and the surroundings. (2) The scene cuts to close-ups of the product display on the warehouse floor, showing the price tags and rows of the 'London to Hogwarts' ticket boxes. (3) The setting shifts to an indoor room where the two women sit at a table. They unbox the large ticket to reveal dozens of smaller, individually wrapped chocolates styled as mini-tickets. (4) They proceed to unwrap and taste two different types: a white chocolate biscuit and a dark chocolate biscuit. They show the details of the '9 3/4' platform logo embossed on the chocolate and give enthusiastic reactions with thumbs-up gestures.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) A fast-paced, upbeat electronic pop track serves as the background music. A male narrator's voice, energetic and high-pitched, introduces the product in Mandarin. Ambient warehouse chatter and the distant hum of a crowd are audible. (2) As the scene transitions home, the narration becomes more focused on descriptive details of the taste. Sound effects (SFX) like a 'ding' for emphasis and a crisp 'crunch' sound when the chocolate is bitten are added. (3) The music maintains its high tempo through the tasting and review phase, concluding with a final upbeat flourish as they recommend the product.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: Large 'COSTCO WHOLESALE' logo at the top. Subtitles throughout the video translate the spoken Mandarin review. (2) Decorative/Hook Text: 'Discussion is exploding', 'Harry Potter Giant Train Ticket Chocolate', 'Price: 649 Yuan/bag', '27 Yuan each', 'Delicious!', 'Higher than expected', 'Worth buying!' (3) Content & Tone: The text uses bright, colorful variety-show style fonts with emojis to create a sense of excitement and urgency.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro in warehouse -> Detailed product display -> Unboxing at home -> Tasting and final verdict.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main female creators, approximately 5 passers-by visible in the background of the Costco scenes.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two young women; one wearing a brown off-shoulder top and a grey bucket hat, the other wearing a similar brown off-shoulder top with long straight black hair.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The first half is set inside a Costco with tall shelves, bulk products, and other shoppers. The second half is a domestic indoor setting with a table, toys, and a sofa visible in the background.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright indoor lighting; fluorescent in the store and warm LED at home.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Product Identification -> Features -> Experience -> Verdict
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a shopping guide and product review. It aims to generate excitement around a novel, large-scale Harry Potter themed chocolate biscuit found at Costco, highlighting its novelty factor (the giant ticket packaging), price-value ratio, and specific taste characteristics (texture and sweetness) to encourage viewers to purchase it.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Detailed pricing information, focus on brand logos, and a clear call-to-action regarding the product's value.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: High-density colorful text overlays and fast-paced 'variety show' style editing.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video leverages the 'Harry Potter' IP and the popularity of 'Costco finds' trends on social media to capture attention. The discrepancy between the giant outer package and the many small inner packages is a key engagement point.
